headline: VC Tax Incentives Attract RM2.8 Billion in New Commitments
context: Tax incentives introduced in 2024 attracted RM2.8 billion in new venture capital fund commitments to Malaysian startups.

cards.big: VC tax incentives attracted RM2.8 billion in new commitments since 2024
cards.sub: Tax breaks on gains and losses encourage institutional startup investment

cards.big: 18 new VC funds established in 2025 managing combined RM4.5 billion
cards.sub: Largest fund at RM800 million focuses on deep tech including semiconductors

cards.big: Foreign VC participation grew 55% with Singapore and Japan most active
cards.sub: Cross-border investment benefited from double taxation agreement network

cards.big: Only 8% of investment went to female-founded startups despite 35% of founders
cards.sub: Gender disparity persists despite government matching incentives for women

cards.big: VC incentives test whether startup funding can be directed toward diversity and geography
RM2.8 billion VC tax incentives. Tax breaks work when they fund innovation, not financial engineering.
